seoTitle: San Remo vs Sandsprings — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between San Remo and Sandsprings. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: San Remo vs Sandsprings: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in San Remo and Sandsprings.
heading: San Remo vs Sandsprings — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between San Remo, Western Australia and Sandsprings, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sandsprings has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to San Remo (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is San Remo or Sandsprings safer from flooding?
faq.answer: San Remo has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Sandsprings scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: San Remo scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Sandsprings scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in San Remo or Sandsprings?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sandsprings has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
